ddls:
CREATE TABLE "neighborhoods" (
  "neighborhood_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each neighborhood */,
  "neighborhood_name" TEXT /* Name of the neighborhood */,
  "population" REAL /* Population of the neighborhood */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the neighborhood in square kilometers */,
  "average_income" REAL /* Average income of residents in the neighborhood */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the neighborhood */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the neighborhood */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the neighborhood */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("neighborhood_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service (e.g., American Restaurant, Bakery) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the service (e.g., Food, Retail, Recreation, Transportation) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the service */,
  "icon_url" TEXT /* URL of an icon representing the service type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_availability" (
  "availability_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service availability record */,
  "neighborhood_id" INTEGER /* ID of the neighborhood */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Number of instances of the service in the neighborhood */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last date when the service count was updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the service (e.g., Active, Inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("availability_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_availability_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_availability_neighborhood_id FOREIGN KEY ("neighborhood_id") REFERENCES neighborhoods ("neighborhood_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "restaurants" (
  "restaurant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each restaurant */,
  "neighborhood_id" INTEGER /* ID of the neighborhood where the restaurant is located */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the restaurant */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of cuisine served */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the restaurant */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the restaurant */,
  "rating" REAL /* Average rating of the restaurant */,
  "operating_hours" TEXT /* Operating hours of the restaurant */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL of the restaurant's website */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("restaurant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_restaurants_neighborhood_id FOREIGN KEY ("neighborhood_id") REFERENCES neighborhoods ("neighborhood_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "retail" (
  "retail_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each retail outlet */,
  "neighborhood_id" INTEGER /* ID of the neighborhood where the outlet is located */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the retail outlet */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of goods sold */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the retail outlet */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the retail outlet */,
  "rating" REAL /* Average rating of the retail outlet */,
  "operating_hours" TEXT /* Operating hours of the retail outlet */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL of the retail outlet's website */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("retail_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_retail_neighborhood_id FOREIGN KEY ("neighborhood_id") REFERENCES neighborhoods ("neighborhood_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "recreation" (
  "facility_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each recreational facility */,
  "neighborhood_id" INTEGER /* ID of the neighborhood where the facility is located */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the recreational facility */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of facility (e.g., Gym, Pool) */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the recreational facility */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the recreational facility */,
  "rating" REAL /* Average rating of the recreational facility */,
  "operating_hours" TEXT /* Operating hours of the recreational facility */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL of the recreational facility's website */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("facility_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_recreation_neighborhood_id FOREIGN KEY ("neighborhood_id") REFERENCES neighborhoods ("neighborhood_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "public_transport" (
  "transport_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each public transportation option */,
  "neighborhood_id" INTEGER /* ID of the neighborhood where the option is available */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the public transportation option */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of transportation (e.g., Bus Line, Subway) */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the public transportation option */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the public transportation option */,
  "schedule_url" TEXT /* URL of the transportation schedule */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL of the public transportation option's website */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transport_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_public_transport_neighborhood_id FOREIGN KEY ("neighborhood_id") REFERENCES neighborhoods ("neighborhood_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., resident, city planner) */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date when the user registered */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last date when the user logged in */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., Active, Inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "neighborhood_id" INTEGER /* ID of the neighborhood being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the neighborhood */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the neighborhood was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, search) */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the specific service being accessed */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used for access (e.g., Mobile, Desktop) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_neighborhood_id FOREIGN KEY ("neighborhood_id") REFERENCES neighborhoods ("neighborhood_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
